title: Chota Mouse and his birthday cake

content.problemStatement:
Today is Chota Mouse's birthday. He wants to celebrate it in a grand style. His brother Mota Chuha immediately contacted a nearby bakery for an online delivery of a cake. The cake is of rectangular shape with height **H** and width **W**. Chota Mouse has invited **F** of his friends to the party. So, he wants to make **F** vertical cuts at distinct positions on the cake, so that the cake will be divided into **F + 1** non-zero area parts (**F** for his friends and one for himself).

Let us assume the cake is placed along the coordinate axis with its left end at **x** coordinate 0 and right end at **W**. Please see the image in the examples for details.

He will make cuts at positions **Pi** such that **Pi** is strictly greater than zero and strictly less than **W**.

Also note that, all the positions for cutting are distinct.

Chota Mouse is a nice guy. So, he will be keeping the piece with the least area for himself and leave the other pieces for his friends to enjoy.

Chota Mouse is weak in mathematics, so can you please help him figure out the area of the piece he is going to have?


Input Format
The first line of the input contains an integer **T** denoting the number of test cases. The description of **T** test cases follows.


First line of each test case contains three space separated integers **F, H, W**.


Second line of each test case contains **F** space separated integers, i-th of them denotes **Pi**.



Output Format
For each test case, print a single integer corresponding to the answer of the problem.
content.inputFormat: The first line of the input contains an integer T denoting the number of test cases. The description of T test cases follows. The first line of each test case contains three space-separated integers F, H, W. The second line of each test case contains F space-separated integers, i-th of them denotes Pi.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single integer corresponding to the area of the piece Chota Mouse is going to have.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Explanation: In the first example, both the pieces have the same area (i.e., = 1). In the second example, there are two pieces. The first piece has an area of 12, and the second has an area of 8. So, the answer will be 8. In the third example, there are three pieces. The first piece has an area of 6, the second 12, and the third 9. So, the answer will be 6.

solution.approach: To find the area of the piece Chota Mouse is going to have, we first need to calculate the areas of all the pieces after making the cuts. We can do this by sorting the cut positions and then calculating the area of each piece by multiplying the height of the cake with the width of the piece. The width of each piece can be found by subtracting the position of the previous cut from the position of the current cut. Finally, we find the minimum area among all the pieces, which will be the area of the piece Chota Mouse is going to have.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * F log F) — where T is the number of test cases and F is the number of cuts. The sorting operation dominates the time complexity.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(F) — for storing the cut positions.

code.Python3:
import sys
T = int(input())
for _ in range(T):
    F, H, W = map(int, input().split())
    P = sorted(map(int, input().split()))
    minArea = H * P[0]
    for i in range(1, F):
        area = H * (P[i] - P[i-1])
        if area < minArea:
            minArea = area
    area = H * (W - P[-1])
    if area < minArea:
        minArea = area
    print(minArea)
